name: tgb

env_axis.name: time
env_axis.shift_mechanism: future-as-test; each sub-dataset has different underlying shift mechanism (Wikipedia growth, trade-policy regime change, etc.)

schema.suite_note: suite — sub-datasets include tgbl-wiki, tgbl-review, tgbl-flight, tgbl-comment, tgbn-trade, tgbn-genre, tgbn-reddit. Each has its own profile and pair. CURRENTLY MATERIALIZED: tgbl-wiki, tgbn-trade, tgbn-genre. PENDING: tgbl-review, tgbl-flight, tgbl-comment, tgbn-reddit.

facts: 7+ sub-datasets (tgbl-wiki, tgbl-review, tgbn-trade, tgbn-genre, tgbl-flight, …); chronological 70/15/15 split; NeurIPS 2023 baselines.
hypotheses: Per sub-dataset, 'future-as-test' is genuinely OOD rather than a longer tail of a stationary process, so a non-OOD baseline is the right comparison.
Method ranking does not transfer across TGB sub-datasets; each is its own incompatible regime.
intuitions: A TGB 2.0 release has likely shipped.
Concept-drift detection literature has probably been applied explicitly to dynamic-link prediction since 2023.
unknowns: Whether the temporal environment axis is single (time) or compound (time × sub-dataset × edge-type) cannot be determined; the suite's structure presupposes time-as-primary.
Each sub-dataset's shift mechanism differs (Wikipedia growth is not trade-policy regime change), so treating them under one banner may be a category error.
epistemic_state.boundary: A suite requiring descent to individual sub-datasets. H=1 (flat within each sub-dataset); C=0. The assumption that the sub-datasets are homogeneous is itself a limitation.

representational_commitments: Each sub-dataset selected for distinct temporal shift mechanism
Edge-level temporal stamps; node-stability across time assumed
